# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim-buster

# Install system packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    libffi6 \
    libpq5 \
    libssl1.0.0 \
    zlib1g \
    libcrypt1 \
    libgcc1 \
    libstdc++6 \
    liblzma5 \
    libbz2-1.0 \
    libreadline7 \
    libsqlite3-dev \
    tk-dev \
    libncurses5:i386 \
    libncursesw5:i386 \
    libffi-dev \
    libpq-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libzip-dev \
    liblzma-dev \
    libbz2-dev \
    libreadline-dev \
    sqlite3 \
    wget

# Install virtualenv and pip
RUN pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel virtualenv

# Create a virtual environment and activate it
RUN virtualenv venv && source venv/bin/activate

# Copy the application code into container
COPY . /app

# Install legacy dependencies listed in requirements.txt
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

# Exit the virtualenv and make sure everything is copied to the correct location
DEPENDENCY_INSTALL_DIR=/app
deactivate

# Workdir
WORKDIR $DEPENDENCY_INSTALL_DIR

# Entry point
CMD ["python"]
